title: Grown-Ups Are Weird
lyrics:
[verse 1]
Dad’s cup puffs *psssh* with steam so high.
He stirs it round—*clink*—but won’t try.
The clock goes *tick*, the time says eight.
Why’s coffee there just to say “Hi”?

[chorus]
Grown-ups are weird, grown-ups are strange.
They do things that flip my brain!
I watch them close, I watch them long,
but what they do don’t feel quite right!

[verse 2]
He taps his phone, *beep-beep*, sings low.
The cup just sits there cold as snow.
If I left milk, he’d say, “Oh no!”
So why’s his cup still sitting there?

[chorus]
Grown-ups are weird, grown-ups are strange.
They do things that flip my brain!
I watch them close, I watch them long,
but what they do don’t feel quite right!

[ending]
Maybe tomorrow I’ll ask why…
Or just dream cups that talk back *bye-bye*.
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, full musical production, acoustic guitar, bass, and beatbox, 120 BPM, super bouncy, high energy, strong groove, clear warm vocal, strong singalong chorus, every word easy to understand
scene: The kitchen clock ticks past 8:30 PM, its hands glowing faintly in the dim light, while Dad stirs his coffee with one hand and scrolls through his phone with the other—both steaming, both ignored. "Wait... why does he even *have* a cup if he’s not gonna drink it?" the child thinks, staring at the abandoned mug, the forgotten warmth, the grown-up world suddenly strange.
cover_description: A kid peeks around a kitchen door at Dad stirring a steaming cup while scrolling his phone, clock hands glowing at 8:30.
